findings.description: Two-column slide layout is completely lost.
findings.evidence: The PDF features a left column for troubleshooting and a right column for the welcome message. The HTML linearizes this, placing the visual layout elements at the top and all text sequentially at the bottom.

findings.description: Text content is duplicated as both a cropped image and HTML text.
findings.evidence: The HTML renders a large cropped portion of the slide (including the troubleshooting text and screenshots) as a static image at the top, and then repeats the slide's text as standard HTML text below it.

findings.description: Side-by-side layout of table and images is lost.
findings.evidence: The PDF displays a timeline table on the left and a vertical strip of five images on the right. The HTML extracts these images and stacks them vertically, destroying the spatial relationship.

findings.description: Sidebar images are placed before the main content.
findings.evidence: The HTML places the entire strip of right-aligned images before the main timeline table in the linear reading flow, interrupting the flow from the title to the table.

findings.description: Multiple <h1> tags are used in the HTML document, whereas guidelines specify that only one <h1> is acceptable as the document title.
findings.evidence:
The HTML contains

Welcome to the CHPP Steering Committee Meeting

,

Agenda

, and

Coastal Habitat Protection Plan

.
